text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real yet-another-react-lightbox repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: this library's root component figures out whether the page/container it is rendered into uses right-to-left (RTL) text direction, and uses that to decide things like which arrow key means 'next slide'. It detects the direction by reading the computed CSS `direction` of its own root element (via `getComputedStyle`). The problem is that this detection only ever runs once, at the moment the lightbox's root element is first attached to the DOM — the result is then cached in component state for the lifetime of that mount and never rechecked. So if whatever controls the computed direction changes later — for example an ancestor element's `dir` attribute is toggled from `ltr` to `rtl` at runtime, after the lightbox has already mounted — the lightbox does not notice. It keeps behaving as if the direction were still whatever it detected at mount time (e.g. arrow keys keep working in the old direction's sense) instead of picking up the new, current direction.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (Node, npm). You can scope the existing Vitest suite to whatever file you're iterating on with `npx vitest run <path-to-file>`. Fix the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

text: I’ll trace the root component’s direction state and its existing tests, then update the source so direction changes after mount are observed without regressing the initial DOM timing. I’ll run the narrowest relevant Vitest coverage first and then the broader checks available for the affected package.

text: The stale value is localized to `LightboxRoot`: the callback ref computes RTL once and stores it in state. The affected behavior is consumed through context, so the fix should live at the root boundary and re-evaluate when relevant DOM direction attributes change, including ancestors outside the lightbox subtree. I’m checking the project’s browser-compatibility and hook patterns before editing.

text: I’ll add a small observer owned by `LightboxRoot`. It will recompute immediately on attachment, watch `dir`, `class`, and `style` changes in the root’s owner document, and disconnect on ref detachment. Watching the owner document’s subtree is necessary because the changed attribute can belong to an ancestor outside the lightbox element; the state update remains guarded by React’s normal equality bailout. I’ll also add a regression test that toggles an ancestor from LTR to RTL after mount.

text: The source and regression test are patched. The test now verifies both phases: the mounted lightbox initially follows LTR keyboard semantics, then the ancestor’s `dir` changes and the same mounted instance follows RTL semantics. I’m running that focused suite now.

The first focused run caught a test-only syntax issue: the new test uses an awaited `act` callback but the test declaration was still synchronous. I’m correcting that declaration and rerunning; the library source has not needed another change.

The focused RTL suite now passes all 11 tests, including the new post-mount direction-change case. I’m doing the remaining validation on the type checker and lint, plus reviewing the final diff for cleanup behavior and compatibility details.
